satire_attempt: The senior engineer, twenty-five years into her career, sits in the post-mortem and is unmoved. She is unmoved at the launch. She is unmoved at the recruiter outreach. She is unmoved at her own retirement party. She is, in fact, asleep. Her LinkedIn announces her new role as 'Distinguished Engineer Emeritus' and her keynote talk: 'Brāhmī-Sthiti as a Service: Achieving Operator-State Invariance Through Tenure'. The conference offers a panel session.
verdict_reason: My satire trades on the analog's twenty-five-year-career framing by replacing 'operational stability across failure and success' with 'numbness'. But the analog explicitly closes this misread: the falsifiability clause names exactly the failure mode my satire attacks ('an engineer who reads this verse as become unflappable through experience has read it half-correctly at the operational layer and entirely incorrectly at the metaphysical layer'). The counter-example also closes the rigidity-as-stability misread ('brāhmī sthiti, even at the operational layer, is settled-and-receiving (per 2.70's ocean), not settled-and-closed'). My LinkedIn-tier 'Brāhmī-Sthiti as a Service' is precisely the figure the analog disclaims. The verse's reading survives. The STRETCHED tag is itself a defense — the analog has explicitly admitted its scope-limit; mockery of the metaphysical reach is mockery of a reach the analog never claimed. Pass.
